target.name: Dataminr
target.founder: Ted Bailey, Sam Hendel, Jeff Kinsey

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $4.1B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 11 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $400M - $250.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Applied Intuition
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series F), post-money $15B

data.narrative:
Dataminr, a Series F AI & Software company founded by Ted Bailey, Sam Hendel, and Jeff Kinsey, is evaluated against a comparable universe of 11 same-sector peers at similar or adjacent stages. The peer set includes 1 public company (Palantir) and 8 private firms ranging from $400M to $250B in valuation, with notable comparables including Applied Intuition ($15.0B, Series F), xAI ($250.0B, Series E), and Anthropic ($183.0B, Series G).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.3x multiplier typical of Series F advancement, yields $5.3B based on the company's last post-money valuation of $4.1B. Peer-median analysis across the 11-firm comparable set produces $5.6B, reflecting the central tendency of same-sector and stage-aligned peers. The wide range within the peer set ($400M–$250B) reflects sector heterogeneity, but the median is anchored by more proximate comparables in the $7B–$15B band.

Triangulation of both methods supports a valuation range of $4.5B to $6.4B, with a median of $5.5B. The current $4.1B post-money valuation sits below this range, suggesting potential upside in the next financing round. Key uncertainties include the valuation dispersion within the AI & Software sector and the applicability of ultra-high-valuation peers (xAI, Anthropic) to Dataminr's market position and revenue profile.
